referenceSources.source: replace with file path, URL, or note
observedDetails: replace with observable color, component, density, imagery, or copy detail
prototypeDecisions: replace with token, layout, component, or content decision

assumptionTerms: replace with unconfirmed terms that must remain outside UI
disallowedCarryoverTerms: replace with prior brand, campaign, role, segment, product, and asset terms

structureGeneration.variationSeed: select a variation seed after evidence and archetype comparison; do not inherit a prior case

businessAxis.rationale: Explain the positive business tension that makes this axis credible. Sparse input alone is never a reason to choose a queue.

navigationLogic.rationale: Explain why this navigation follows the selected operating axis and recurring journey rather than a prior case.

homeNarrative.rationale: Explain the order as a response to the selected operating tension, not as a generic workbench stack.

informationDensity.rationale: Show enough evidence for decisions while preserving mobile scan speed.

visualAnchor.rationale: Explain how this anchor comes from reference evidence or an explicit neutral style decision.

signatureInteraction.stateChange: Describe the route, state, handoff, or write-back change caused by the selected interaction.
signatureInteraction.rationale: Explain how the interaction advances this case's business axis rather than adding decorative novelty.

candidateDirections.summary: Candidate A selected from this project's operating tensions
Candidate B selected from this project's operating tensions
Candidate C selected from this project's operating tensions

structureGeneration.selectionRationale: Replace with the business and evidence rationale for the winner; do not select a direction because it is present in this template.

rejectedDirections.reason: Replace with a concrete reason grounded in this project's operating tension.
Replace with a concrete reason grounded in this project's operating tension.

archetypeSelection.operationalTension: Replace with the recurring decision, constraint, and consequence that selects this project's operating model.
archetypeSelection.selectionRationale: Replace with why the selected archetype fits the evidence better than the rejected alternatives.
archetypeSelection.antiConvergenceCommitment: Replace with the navigation, home, detail, and interaction choices that keep this case distinct after brand styling is removed.

rejectedArchetypes.reason: Replace with a concrete rejection reason.
Replace with a concrete rejection reason.

creativeDivergence.creativeThesis: Replace with an operating metaphor that describes how this project organizes real clienteling work without borrowing a prior case.

inspirationTranspositions.referencePattern: Replace with a real reference pattern
inspirationTranspositions.extractedMechanism: Replace with the abstract interaction or information mechanism.
inspirationTranspositions.clientelingTranslation: Replace with a clienteling translation that fits the selected business axis.
inspirationTranspositions.rejectionBoundary: State what is deliberately not copied and which unsupported business claims stay out of UI.

inspirationTranspositions.referencePattern: Replace with a second real reference pattern
inspirationTranspositions.extractedMechanism: Replace with a second abstract interaction or information mechanism.
inspirationTranspositions.clientelingTranslation: Replace with a second clienteling translation that fits the selected business axis.
inspirationTranspositions.rejectionBoundary: State what is deliberately not copied and which unsupported business claims stay out of UI.

divergenceLevers.rationale: Explain why this business tension calls for the selected axis.

divergenceLevers.decision: Replace with the selected top-level model and center-action policy.
divergenceLevers.rationale: Explain why this navigation supports the selected axis.

divergenceLevers.decision: Replace with the selected home narrative and first-viewport order.
divergenceLevers.rationale: Explain why the sequence supports the operating tension.

divergenceLevers.decision: Replace with the distinct detail-page architectures chosen for this case.
divergenceLevers.rationale: Explain why these architectures fit different clienteling jobs.

divergenceLevers.decision: Replace with the selected state-changing interaction.
divergenceLevers.rationale: Explain how the interaction advances the selected operating axis.

portfolioContrast.comparisonTarget: Replace with a real prior/likely comparison case or no-prior-case statement.
portfolioContrast.debrandedDifference: Replace with the business-axis, navigation, page-architecture, and interaction differences that remain without brand styling.

coherenceProof.navigationFollowsAxis: Replace with proof that the selected navigation follows the actual business axis.
coherenceProof.homeSupportsThesis: Replace with proof that the first viewport supports the selected operating metaphor.
coherenceProof.detailArchitecturesFit: Replace with proof that each full-detail page's structure fits its job.
coherenceProof.signatureInteractionChangesWork: Replace with proof of a route, state, handoff, or result change.
coherenceProof.workbenchStillScannable: Replace with proof that repeated work surfaces remain legible and fast.
coherenceProof.evidenceBoundaryRespected: Replace with proof that unsupported internal terms and claims remain out of UI.

typography.fontFamily: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, SF Pro Display, PingFang SC, Microsoft YaHei, sans-serif
typography.hierarchyNotes: Use compact operational hierarchy; reserve expressive brand display treatment for moments that do not reduce workbench scanning speed.

display.size: 28-40px according to brand evidence and screen density
display.tracking: brand-appropriate; avoid negative tracking for dense Chinese labels
display.lineHeight: 0.96-1.16 according to language and brand tone
display.transform: none, uppercase, lowercase, or title case based on evidence
display.useCases: only for page hero, campaign moment, executive-demo moment, or brand anchor; not for dense lists

h1.useCases: page titles, detail titles, important card titles

h2.useCases: section titles, task titles, list row titles

num.useCases: KPI numbers, money amounts, percentages, countdowns, queue counts

eyebrow.tracking: 0.08em-0.18em when uppercase; 0 for dense Chinese labels
eyebrow.transform: none or uppercase based on evidence
eyebrow.useCases: kicker labels, category tags, source labels, status labels

componentStyle.chipStyle: outlined or soft filled, compact height
componentStyle.tabStyle: segmented or chip tabs according to hierarchy
componentStyle.ctaStyle: solid primary for main action, outline for secondary action
componentStyle.listStyle: dense rows with clear metadata and status chips
componentStyle.filterStyle: search first, then chips or bottom-sheet filters

geometry.controlRadius: brand-appropriate control radius with operational touch targets preserved
geometry.cardRadius: brand-appropriate card radius; avoid changing radius just for decoration
geometry.clipCorner: 0 unless the brand evidence supports cut-corner geometry
geometry.description: Choose the geometry system from evidence and industry fit. Sharp, clipped, soft, rounded, editorial, or precise geometry are all valid when justified; the default is compact and readable.

avoid: using one brand hue for the whole prototype
accentRule.description: Define exactly where the accent is allowed. Strong brand accents should create hierarchy, not replace the workbench information system.

anchor.primaryAnchor: choose the brand's main visual anchor: typography, imagery, color block, product module, metric display, spacing, or material detail
anchor.secondaryAnchor: supporting visual anchor that can repeat across pages without becoming noise
anchor.description: The anchor may be quiet or expressive. Use it to make the brand recognizable while preserving operational scanning.

navigationModel.orderRationale: Replace with a journey-frequency and role-based rationale. Do not keep the starter order automatically.
navigationModel.centerAction: none, quick-create, quick-send, scan, or another justified cross-module action
navigationModel.activeTreatment: Describe icon, label, indicator, color, weight, or material treatment based on brand evidence.
navigationModel.roleVariation: Describe whether manager and frontline navigation differ, or explain why they remain the same.

structuralDifferentiation.homeComposition: Choose a specific composition such as queue-first, customer-first, appointment-first, metric-first, campaign-to-task, or manager overview, and explain the information order.
structuralDifferentiation.customerArchitecture: Choose a C360 structure such as tabbed dossier, timeline-led profile, action-first concierge, product-interest canvas, or compact CRM sheet.
structuralDifferentiation.taskArchitecture: Choose a channel-appropriate structure such as checklist execution, target-progress worklist, content-preview workflow, conversation-led action, or appointment conversion.
structuralDifferentiation.appointmentArchitecture: Choose schedule-led, preparation-led, customer-context-led, resource-led, or outcome-led structure.
structuralDifferentiation.dashboardArchitecture: Choose grouped scorecard, exception-first review, ranked team view, funnel-led analysis, or another role-appropriate structure.
structuralDifferentiation.signatureInteraction: Define one useful brand-appropriate interaction that changes the workflow, not only its decoration.
antiTemplateCheck: navigation is intentionally chosen rather than copied from the starter shell
home information order reflects the brand's clienteling model
at least two detail pages use different component structures from the starter shell
signature interaction is visible and functional

deliveryDiscipline.positioning: brand-aware WeCom Clienteling demo prototype plus solution blueprint
selfCritiquePrompts: What is the strongest structural difference from the starter shell?
What is the strongest structural difference from the most similar prior case?
Which module best expresses this brand's clienteling model, and why?
Which page would fail first in a real demo if its information were thinner?
Which visible terms are evidence-backed, and which assumptions were kept out of UI?
If palette, logo, and brand name were removed, what would still make this prototype structurally distinct?
antiGenericFlags: home uses default module order without evidence
detail pages are summary cards plus one CTA
differentiation is only palette, class names, or typography
unconfirmed internal business terms are visible
one card grammar is reused everywhere
debranded prototype is still structurally generic

workbenchBalance.heroPolicy: Use expressive hero or campaign modules only when they clarify today's work, a launch, or an executive demo path.
operationalPriority: search and filters remain immediately visible on list pages
task action, due date, progress, and target grain remain faster to scan than decorative brand elements
appointment time, customer, service, resource, and status remain aligned and legible
dashboard metrics preserve grouping, trend, target, and drilldown clarity
workbenchBalance.accentBudget: Reserve strong brand color or high-contrast blocks for one or two priority moments per page.
workbenchBalance.moduleDifferentiation: At least home, customer list, task list/detail, appointment, and dashboard should have module-appropriate layout differences, not only recolored cards.
workbenchBalance.pageLayerOnly: Brand skin applies to page-layer components and content modules only; protected shell and native WeCom replicas remain structurally neutral.
readabilityRules: no brand treatment that hides disabled, error, no-permission, or success states
no decorative imagery where business data is missing

home: first viewport includes at least two workbench signals: priority clients, tasks, appointments, dashboard KPI, or campaign-to-task entry
brand hero or campaign module does not consume the entire first viewport unless an adjacent workbench summary remains visible
customers: search input is visible before customer rows
filter or segment controls are visible before customer rows
each row shows customer identity, status/segment, next action or reason, and due/priority signal
tasks: task filters or tabs are visible before task rows
each task row shows source/type, target grain, due/progress, and primary next action
native WeCom handoff task is visually distinct when present
appointments: time, customer, service/resource, owner, and status are aligned and legible
create/confirm/follow-up action is visible when the page is interactive
dashboard: KPI groups preserve metric, target/trend, period, and drilldown entry
dashboard does not use decorative hero analytics as the primary structure
nativeWeCom: native compose/send pages stay structurally neutral
recipient count, message, attachment, frequency note, send/cancel, and return state remain visible

allowedAssets: neutral placeholder blocks when assets are missing
imageryRules.fallback: Use text-led operational cards instead of stock-like images when no approved asset exists.

protectedShell: bottom navigation mechanics and safe area
adaptablePageLayer: bottom navigation item set, order, labels, icons, active treatment, and optional center action
shellBoundary.nativeReplicaTreatment: Keep native WeCom pages neutral and native-like; do not brand-redesign them.

moduleAdaptation.home: Use brand skin on metrics, priority sections, and imagery only when it supports daily work.
moduleAdaptation.customers: Keep search/filter prominent; adapt chips and row rhythm to brand density.
moduleAdaptation.c360: Use brand tone for customer summary and preference/product-interest sections.
moduleAdaptation.tasks: Keep execution clarity first; brand only chips, progress, and supporting content cards.
moduleAdaptation.appointments: Show service/resource states with compact cards and clear CTAs.
moduleAdaptation.content: Use product or campaign imagery when approved.
moduleAdaptation.dashboard: Use operational KPI grouping; avoid decorative hero analytics.
moduleAdaptation.transfer: Keep ownership, permission, and result states clear.
moduleAdaptation.extensions: Visually distinguish extension modules without breaking shell consistency.

avoid: generic repeated cards with only color changes
assumptions: Replace with inferred choices and unresolved visual questions.
